info.name: Amazon Neptune Management API
DB Clusters
Amazon Neptune Describe Neptune DB Clusters

params.name: DBClusterIdentifier
params.description: The identifier of the DB cluster to describe.

params.name: MaxRecords
params.description: Maximum number of records to include in the response (20-100).

params.name: Marker
params.description: Pagination token from a previous request.

items.docs: Returns information about provisioned Neptune DB clusters. If a cluster identifier is supplied, details for that specific cluster are returned. Otherwise, details for up to 100 clusters are returned.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Create a New Neptune DB Cluster

items.docs: Creates a new Amazon Neptune DB cluster. You can use the ReplicationSourceIdentifier parameter to create the DB cluster as a read replica of another DB cluster.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Delete a Neptune DB Cluster

params.name: DBClusterIdentifier
params.description: The DB cluster identifier for the cluster to be deleted.

params.name: SkipFinalSnapshot
params.description: Whether to skip creation of a final snapshot before deletion.

params.name: FinalDBSnapshotIdentifier
params.description: The identifier of the final snapshot to create before deletion.

items.docs: Deletes a previously provisioned DB cluster. When you delete a DB cluster, all automated backups are deleted and cannot be recovered. Manual snapshots are not deleted.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Modify a Neptune DB Cluster

items.docs: Modifies settings for a DB cluster. You can change one or more database configuration parameters by specifying them and their new values.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Start a Stopped Neptune DB Cluster

params.name: DBClusterIdentifier
params.description: The identifier of the DB cluster to start.

items.docs: Starts a stopped Neptune DB cluster. When you start a cluster, Neptune restores the cluster to the state it was in when it was stopped.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Stop a Running Neptune DB Cluster

params.name: DBClusterIdentifier
params.description: The identifier of the DB cluster to stop.

items.docs: Stops a running Neptune DB cluster. While the cluster is stopped, you are charged only for storage. A stopped cluster automatically restarts after 7 days.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Force a Failover for a Neptune DB Cluster

params.name: DBClusterIdentifier
params.description: The identifier of the cluster to force a failover for.

params.name: TargetDBInstanceIdentifier
params.description: The name of the instance to promote to the primary instance.

items.docs: Forces a failover for a DB cluster. A failover for a DB cluster promotes one of the read replicas in the cluster to be the new primary instance.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Associate an IAM Role with a Neptune DB Cluster

params.name: DBClusterIdentifier
params.description: The name of the DB cluster to associate the IAM role with.

params.name: RoleArn
params.description: The ARN of the IAM role to associate with the cluster.

items.docs: Associates an Identity and Access Management (IAM) role with a Neptune DB cluster so that the cluster can access other AWS services on your behalf.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Disassociate an IAM Role from a Neptune DB Cluster

params.name: DBClusterIdentifier
params.description: The name of the DB cluster to disassociate the role from.

params.name: RoleArn
params.description: The ARN of the IAM role to disassociate.

items.docs: Disassociates an Identity and Access Management (IAM) role from a Neptune DB cluster.

info.name: DB Instances
Amazon Neptune Create a New Neptune DB Instance

items.docs: Creates a new DB instance in a Neptune DB cluster. The DB instance inherits the cluster's storage and high availability configuration.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Delete a Neptune DB Instance

params.name: DBInstanceIdentifier
params.description: The DB instance identifier for the instance to be deleted.

params.name: SkipFinalSnapshot
params.description: Whether to skip creation of a final snapshot.

items.docs: Deletes a previously provisioned DB instance. When you delete an instance, all automated backups for that instance are deleted and cannot be recovered.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Describe Neptune DB Instances

params.name: DBInstanceIdentifier
params.description: The identifier of the DB instance to describe.

params.name: MaxRecords
params.description: Maximum number of records to include in the response.

params.name: Marker
params.description: Pagination token from a previous request.

items.docs: Returns information about provisioned Neptune DB instances. If a specific instance identifier is supplied, details for that instance are returned.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Modify a Neptune DB Instance

items.docs: Modifies settings for a DB instance. You can change one or more database configuration parameters by specifying them and their new values.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Reboot a Neptune DB Instance

params.name: DBInstanceIdentifier
params.description: The identifier of the DB instance to reboot.

params.name: ForceFailover
params.description: Whether to force a failover during the reboot.

items.docs: Reboots a DB instance. When you reboot an instance, the instance is restarted. Rebooting results in a momentary outage.

info.name: Snapshots
Amazon Neptune Create a Snapshot of a Neptune DB Cluster

params.name: DBClusterIdentifier
params.description: The identifier of the DB cluster to create a snapshot for.

params.name: DBClusterSnapshotIdentifier
params.description: The identifier for the DB cluster snapshot.

items.docs: Creates a snapshot of a DB cluster. Manual snapshots can be used to restore a DB cluster to a specific state.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Describe Neptune DB Cluster Snapshots

params.name: DBClusterSnapshotIdentifier
params.description: The identifier of a specific snapshot to describe.

params.name: DBClusterIdentifier
params.description: The identifier of the cluster to filter snapshots by.

params.name: MaxRecords
params.description: Maximum number of records to include in the response.

params.name: Marker
params.description: Pagination token from a previous request.

items.docs: Returns information about DB cluster snapshots. If a specific snapshot identifier is supplied, details for that snapshot are returned.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Delete a Neptune DB Cluster Snapshot

params.name: DBClusterSnapshotIdentifier
params.description: The identifier of the cluster snapshot to delete.

items.docs: Deletes a DB cluster snapshot. If the snapshot is being copied, the copy operation is terminated.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Restore a Neptune DB Cluster from a Snapshot

items.docs: Creates a new DB cluster from a DB snapshot or DB cluster snapshot. The new DB cluster is created from the source snapshot with a default configuration.

info.name: Subnet Groups
Amazon Neptune Create a New DB Subnet Group

params.name: DBSubnetGroupName
params.description: The name for the DB subnet group.

params.name: DBSubnetGroupDescription
params.description: The description for the DB subnet group.

items.docs: Creates a new DB subnet group. DB subnet groups must contain at least one subnet in at least two Availability Zones in the AWS Region.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Describe DB Subnet Groups

params.name: DBSubnetGroupName
params.description: The name of a specific DB subnet group to describe.

items.docs: Returns a list of DB subnet group descriptions. If a specific subnet group name is supplied, the description for that group is returned.

info.name: Parameter Groups
Amazon Neptune Create a New DB Parameter Group

params.name: DBParameterGroupName
params.description: The name of the DB parameter group.

params.name: DBParameterGroupFamily
params.description: The DB parameter group family name (e.g., neptune1, neptune1.2, neptune1.3).

params.name: Description
params.description: The description for the DB parameter group.

items.docs: Creates a new DB parameter group. A DB parameter group is initially created with the default parameters for the database engine used.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Create a New DB Cluster Parameter Group

params.name: DBClusterParameterGroupName
params.description: The name of the DB cluster parameter group.

params.name: DBParameterGroupFamily
params.description: The DB parameter group family name.

params.name: Description
params.description: The description for the DB cluster parameter group.

items.docs: Creates a new DB cluster parameter group. Parameters in a DB cluster parameter group apply to all of the instances in a DB cluster.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Describe DB Parameter Groups

params.name: DBParameterGroupName
params.description: The name of a specific DB parameter group to describe.

items.docs: Returns a list of DB parameter group descriptions.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Describe DB Cluster Parameter Groups

params.name: DBClusterParameterGroupName
params.description: The name of a specific cluster parameter group to describe.

items.docs: Returns a list of DB cluster parameter group descriptions.

info.name: Events
Amazon Neptune Create a Neptune Event Notification Subscription

params.name: SubscriptionName
params.description: The name of the subscription.

params.name: SnsTopicArn
params.description: The ARN of the SNS topic to send notifications to.

params.name: SourceType
params.description: The type of source that triggers notifications (db-instance, db-cluster, db-parameter-group, db-cluster-snapshot).

params.name: Enabled
params.description: Whether the subscription is enabled.

items.docs: Creates an event notification subscription. This action requires an Amazon SNS topic ARN. Notifications are sent to the SNS topic when specified events occur.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Describe Neptune Events

params.name: SourceIdentifier
params.description: The identifier of the event source to filter events.

params.name: SourceType
params.description: The event source type to filter events.

params.name: Duration
params.description: The number of minutes to retrieve events for.

items.docs: Returns events related to DB instances, DB clusters, DB parameter groups, and DB cluster snapshots for the past 14 days.

info.name: Default
Amazon Neptune Add Tags to a Neptune Resource

params.name: ResourceName
params.description: The ARN of the resource to add tags to.

items.docs: Adds metadata tags to an Amazon Neptune resource. Tags are key-value pairs that you can use to categorize and manage your resources.

info.name: Amazon Neptune List Tags for a Neptune Resource

params.name: ResourceName
params.description: The ARN of the resource to list tags for.

items.docs: Lists all tags on an Amazon Neptune resource.

info.name: Engine
Amazon Neptune Describe Neptune DB Engine Versions

params.name: Engine
params.description: The database engine to return versions for.

params.name: EngineVersion
params.description: A specific engine version to return details for.

params.name: DBParameterGroupFamily
params.description: The parameter group family to filter versions by.

items.docs: Returns a list of the available DB engine versions. The output shows the supported features and parameter group families for each version.

info.name: Global Clusters
Amazon Neptune Create a Neptune Global Database Cluster

params.name: GlobalClusterIdentifier
params.description: The cluster identifier of the new global cluster.

params.name: SourceDBClusterIdentifier
params.description: The ARN of an existing Neptune cluster to use as the primary cluster.

params.name: DeletionProtection
params.description: Whether the global cluster has deletion protection enabled.

items.docs: Creates a Neptune global database spread across multiple AWS Regions. The global database contains a single primary cluster with read-write capability.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Describe Neptune Global Database Clusters

params.name: GlobalClusterIdentifier
params.description: The identifier of the global cluster to describe.

items.docs: Returns information about Neptune global database clusters.

info.name: Maintenance
Amazon Neptune Describe Pending Maintenance Actions

params.name: ResourceIdentifier
params.description: The ARN of a resource to filter maintenance actions.

items.docs: Returns a list of resources with pending maintenance actions.

info.name: Amazon Neptune Apply a Pending Maintenance Action

params.name: ResourceIdentifier
params.description: The ARN of the resource to apply the action to.

params.name: ApplyAction
params.description: The pending maintenance action to apply.

params.name: OptInType
params.description: The type of opt-in request (immediate, next-maintenance, undo-opt-in).

items.docs: Applies a pending maintenance action to a resource.
